evidence: User said 'lets run then consilium and do all recomended steps' — minimal instruction, trusts system to self-direct
Project is React 19 + TypeScript + Vite + SCSS Modules + Storybook + Vitest
Claude Code memory: feedback_visual_qa.md states always verify components visually via Playwright screenshots
Global CLAUDE.md contains explicit angry feedback about unauthorized deletion of Docker containers
Owner uses Russian for casual conversation ('замержил', 'что осталось дальше', 'го пока что') and English for technical context
Owner said 'можешь сам замержить этот пул реквест' — trusts agent to merge PRs without review
Owner requested v0.1.* branches with last 5 retention, tag-based releases, GitHub Packages publishing
Owner asked 'локально как посмотреть сначала чтоб я подтвердил' — wants to verify locally before approving
When asked GitHub Packages vs npmjs.org, owner said 'го пока что GitHub Packages'
Owner repeatedly said 'давай все' and 'lets cover all remaining discovered stuff too' — prefers doing everything at once over incremental
Owner described 15+ desired features in a single message: i18n, currency, docs, pages, components, CRUD, flows, reusability. Prefers describing the full vision at once.
Owner uses strong language when frustrated: 'бро чот какая то' (about broken MDX). Not hostile — expressing urgency. Respond with immediate action, not apologies.
Owner immediately reported Storybook MDX loading error and demanded parallel fix agent. Visual verification in Storybook is critical — broken pages are unacceptable.
Owner's feature request covered: i18n components, currency switcher, CRUD flows, e-commerce journey, onboarding, pre-order, search, filters, reusable components. Prefers ambitious scope over minimal.
Owner got frustrated when ThemeSwitcher didn't work visually and MDX tables were broken. Not interested in 'it compiles' — must look and work correctly.
Owner said 'такое ощущение что херово консиллум не парился' — wants agents that think like real IT giant teams, not checkbox-checkers. Thoroughness over speed.
Owner trusts autonomous PR merging and wants things committed and pushed immediately. No need to ask for permission on routine commits.
Owner reacts with frustration when delivered output is broken or incomplete. Expects first delivery to work. Rework is unacceptable.

Owner provided screenshots of Uzum.uz, Ozon.ru, Alifshop.uz and demands pixel-level match: white bg product images, badge placement at bottom of image, installment pricing in colored pills, full-width CTA buttons, heart wishlist top-right, discount badge top-left. See feedback_modern_design_requirements.md
Owner explicitly requested deletion of all 3 DesktopHomePage variants (A, B, C) — likely unsatisfied with quality. Desktop pages need redesign from scratch matching Uzum/Ozon reference.
Requested deep audit of SSR/SEO/reusability. Approved 'use client' + as/href + CSS vars + className merging. Moving toward Next.js deployment.
Got very frustrated about product card height inconsistency. Visual alignment of CTA buttons across grid is non-negotiable.
Explicitly demanded real brand images from Newegg/ASUS, rejected picsum.photos placeholders. Wants Storybook to look like real e-commerce.
Explicitly told to always use team agents, not standalone agents. Frustrated when agents were launched outside team.
Very angry when agents produced identical stories with different names but same data. Expects genuinely different content per story variant.

Frustrated when Desktop components end up in wrong Storybook sidebar sections. Components must be grouped logically: Desktop/{Category}/{ComponentName}. Disorganized Storybook = unprofessional library.
Frustrated when agents create new components instead of reusing existing ones from the library. Always check barrel exports and existing components before creating anything new.

v5.1.0 branch shows pattern: modify SCSS for DesktopDialog, DesktopModal, DesktopPopup + refine pages (checkout, login, register, product detail). Owner iterates on visual polish of existing components, not just creating new ones.

Current WIP adds 6 new components (DesktopDeliverySelector, DesktopInstallmentCalculator, DesktopRatingDistribution, DesktopNotificationBell, DesktopColorSwatch, DesktopImageZoom) + 2 new pages alongside visual polish of existing ones. Expansion and refinement happen simultaneously.
Frustrated with agents not following established rules from CLAUDE.md. Rules exist for a reason. Non-compliance wastes time and erodes trust.
Agent admitted audit was incomplete (4/150 components). Owner frustrated but appreciated the transparency. Prefers honest reporting of what was NOT done over silent omissions or excuses.

Owner immediately flagged AliExpress branding in component names. Components published to npm/GitHub must be brand-neutral. Competitor names damage the library's reputation as an independent open-source project.

When owner says 'все' or 'абсолютно все' or 'audit all 150+ components', it means every single component directory. Not just recent changes. Agent audited 4/150 and owner was frustrated. Never scope-reduce the owner's 'all' to 'what I touched'.

Owner frustrated that header was full-width. Showed AliExpress reference — header is inside max-width container with rounded corners, not edge-to-edge. All page content including header should be within max-width container.

Owner provides reference screenshots and expects exact match. Agent repeatedly deviated from reference (full-width header, wrong MegaMenu dropdown, wrong footer borders). Must compare every detail against the reference before committing.
Owner frustrated that design feedback was not recorded in owner profile or design rules. Every correction must be logged in owner-profile.jsonl AND critical-rules.md immediately.

Owner demanded rich stories for ALL mobile+desktop components. 'абсолютно по всем mobile and desktop компонентам пройтись и сделать rich сторисы'. Launched 10 parallel agents, ~168 files, ~1700 stories.

Owner angry about hardcoded orange gradient in Sale Hits bg. 'оранжевый для темы монохром херота подзалупная, серая должна быть нет?' — all component backgrounds MUST use var(--gs-*) that adapts to any theme palette.
Owner noticed 24px padding on Sale Hits making content narrower than header. 'why such clinical moments of design not mentioned and skipped?' — horizontal alignment between page sections is critical.
